The new leg includes performances in North America and a short series of shows in Asia, expanding his global reach.
This announcement reflects Byrne's continued popularity and the demand for live music post-pandemic.

stories.title: Analysis: Warsh emerges from a difficult hearing with his Fed 'regime-change' plan intact
bullets: Fed Chair Warsh emerged from a challenging hearing with his plan for a central bank overhaul still intact.
Despite intense scrutiny over his finances, he faced minimal opposition regarding his proposed 'regime-change' strategy.
The outcome of this hearing is pivotal for the Fed's future direction and monetary policy adjustments.

stories.title: Samsung is heavily discounting its older smart TVs to make room for 2026 stock — save up to $1,600 with these deals!
bullets: Samsung is discounting older smart TVs by up to $1,600 to clear inventory for new 2026 models, effective immediately.
These discounts aim to attract consumers ahead of the summer shopping season, enhancing Samsung's competitive edge in the market.
This strategy may lead to increased sales volume, helping Samsung maintain its leadership position in the electronics industry.

stories.title: The Most Inescapable Voice In Gaming Wants To Start His Own Studio
bullets: Voice actor Troy Baker announced plans to start his own game development studio, shifting from voice acting to production.
Baker aims to create innovative gaming experiences, leveraging his extensive industry knowledge and fanbase support.
This move could reshape his career and influence the gaming landscape, encouraging other voice actors to explore similar paths.

stories.title: AI company deletes the 3 million OKCupid photos it used for facial recognition training
bullets: AI company Clarifai deleted 3 million photos from OkCupid used for facial recognition training, following a settlement with the FTC.
The deletion was certified to the FTC on April 7, 2026, after Match Group faced scrutiny over privacy violations related to user data.
This incident highlights ongoing concerns about data privacy and the ethical use of personal information in AI training.

stories.title: Joan Jett And The Blackhearts announce first UK headline tour in 16 years for summer 2026
bullets: Joan Jett And The Blackhearts announced their first UK headline tour in 16 years, set for summer 2026.
The band expressed excitement about returning to the UK, promising fans an unforgettable experience after a long absence.
This tour could revitalize interest in the band's music and attract new fans, boosting their legacy in the rock genre.

stories.title: Democratic Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick Resigns Ahead of Potential Ethics Sanctions
bullets: Florida Democratic Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick resigned amid potential ethics sanctions for campaign finance violations.
The House Ethics Committee found her guilty, prompting her resignation ahead of possible disciplinary actions.
Her departure raises questions about accountability in Congress and may impact upcoming elections in Florida.

stories.title: Ghost fishing gear: The hidden threat at the bottom of our seas and oceans
bullets: The EU-funded NETTAG+ project aims to reduce ghost fishing gear's impact on marine life by collaborating with fishermen.
This initiative addresses the environmental threat posed by abandoned fishing gear, which harms marine ecosystems and wildlife.
Successful implementation could lead to significant improvements in ocean health and sustainable fishing practices.

stories.title: Report: Meta will train AI agents by tracking employees' mouse, keyboard use
bullets: Meta plans to train AI agents by monitoring employees' mouse and keyboard activity, highlighting data collection challenges.
This approach aims to enhance the quality of interactive training data, crucial for developing effective AI systems.
The strategy raises concerns about employee privacy and the ethical implications of workplace surveillance.

stories.title: Bob Dylan Announces New 2026 Summer Tour Dates
bullets: Bob Dylan announced new summer tour dates for 2026, including performances in major cities like New York and Boston.
The tour aims to celebrate his enduring legacy, with fans eagerly anticipating his live performances across the country.
These concerts could significantly boost ticket sales and further solidify Dylan's status as a music icon.

stories.title: My Dad Used to Give the Best Advice. Now He Has a Two-Word Answer to Every Question. I’m Not Alone.
bullets: A personal reflection reveals a growing disconnect between the author and their father, who now offers only two-word answers.
This change highlights the emotional challenges of aging and communication breakdowns in familial relationships.
Addressing these issues may foster deeper connections and understanding, emphasizing the importance of open dialogue.

stories.title: Chinese court cases reveal most trafficked rhino horns come from Southern Africa
bullets: Recent court cases in China reveal that most trafficked rhino horns originate from Southern Africa, exacerbating conservation issues.
Rhinoceroses are endangered due to poaching driven by demand for their horns in traditional medicine and luxury items.
This alarming trend underscores the urgent need for international cooperation to combat wildlife trafficking and protect endangered species.

stories.title: 'The Last Of Us' Season 3 Could Give One Evil Character Some Much-Needed Depth
bullets: Li Jun Li has been cast as Lev and Yara's mother for Season 3 of 'The Last Of Us', enhancing character depth.
The addition aims to explore the character's backstory, providing a richer narrative experience for viewers.
This casting decision highlights the show's commitment to character development, potentially increasing viewer engagement.

stories.title: Today’s Android app deals and freebies: Rusty Lake Paradise, Out There, Underground Blossom, more
bullets: Today's Android app deals feature discounts on popular titles like Rusty Lake Paradise and Out There, alongside hardware deals.
Samsung Galaxy Ring is discounted by up to $292, while unlocked Pixel 10 sees savings of up to $321.
These promotions aim to boost user engagement and sales, enhancing the Android ecosystem's appeal to consumers.

stories.title: Trump's 2.0 Cabinet welcomed women, but they've been the first to leave
bullets: President Trump's second-term Cabinet included more women, but three have resigned within two months amid scandals.
Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer is among those who left, facing bipartisan pressure over her performance.
The resignations raise concerns about the stability of Trump's Cabinet and the handling of controversies.

stories.title: Exploits Turn Windows Defender into Attacker Tool
bullets: Three proof-of-concept exploits are actively attacking Microsoft's Windows Defender, with two of them currently unpatched as of April 21, 2026.
These exploits allow attackers to bypass security measures, posing a significant risk to users relying on Windows Defender for protection.
Microsoft must address these vulnerabilities urgently to protect millions of users and maintain trust in its security solutions.

stories.title: AI research lab NeoCognition lands $40M seed to build agents that learn like humans
bullets: NeoCognition, founded by an OSU researcher, has secured $40 million in seed funding to develop human-like AI agents.
The funding aims to advance AI capabilities, enabling agents to learn and adapt across various domains.
This investment reflects growing interest in AI technologies that mimic human learning processes.

stories.title: Xbox VP Clarifies A Key Detail About Project Helix, But Questions Remain
bullets: An Xbox VP clarified details about Project Helix, a next-gen platform capable of playing both console and PC games.
Rumors suggested Microsoft might sell Project Helix chips to third-party companies, raising questions about its market position.
The clarification aims to quell speculation, but uncertainty about the device's nature remains among gamers.

stories.title: Why doesn't Xbox Game Pass include every Call of Duty game?
bullets: Microsoft confirmed that new Call of Duty games will not be available on Xbox Game Pass at launch.
This decision has disappointed many subscribers who anticipated immediate access to new titles in the franchise.
The move may impact Game Pass subscriptions as gamers seek alternatives for accessing popular game releases.

stories.title: Framework Has a Better, More Take-Apartable Laptop
bullets: Framework announces the launch of its new Laptop 13 Pro, featuring improved modular design for easy repairs.
The updated 16-inch model also includes enhancements, catering to users seeking customizable and sustainable technology.
This initiative aligns with growing consumer demand for repairable devices, promoting sustainability in the tech industry.

stories.title: Inside the Vikram Sodhi Centre of Excellence: How IIT Kharagpur Plans to Modernise Indian Mining
bullets: IIT Kharagpur launched the Vikram Sodhi Centre of Excellence on April 21, 2026, to modernize Indian mining using AI technologies.
The center's 'Grand Challenges' initiative will utilize real mining data to create actionable solutions, enhancing efficiency and sustainability in the sector.
This initiative aims to bridge the gap between data and practical applications, potentially transforming the Indian mining industry.

stories.title: Cherfilus-McCormick is third lawmaker to quit, as wave of scandal swamps US Congress
bullets: Democratic lawmaker Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick resigned amid a wave of ethics scandals affecting the US Congress.
Her resignation follows Eric Swalwell and another lawmaker, all facing scrutiny over campaign finance violations.
This trend raises concerns about ethical standards in Congress and may influence upcoming elections.

stories.title: I got an early look at ChatGPT Images 2.0, and it's impressive - with one exception
bullets: OpenAI's ChatGPT Images 2.0 offers enhanced precision and design control for users creating visual content.
The new version aims to improve user experience, though it has one notable limitation that remains unaddressed.
This update positions OpenAI as a leader in AI-driven design tools, attracting creative professionals.

stories.title: OpenAI Beefs Up ChatGPT’s Image Generation Model
bullets: OpenAI released the ChatGPT Images 2.0 model on April 21, 2026, improving image detail and text rendering capabilities.
Testing shows the new model excels in English but struggles with other languages, limiting its global usability.
This upgrade is crucial for enhancing user experience and expanding applications in creative industries and digital content creation.

stories.title: ChatGPT Images 2: Why OpenAI Built a New Image Model After Killing Sora
bullets: OpenAI's new image model, ChatGPT Images 2.0, follows the discontinuation of the Sora model, emphasizing creative applications.
The company aims to integrate this model into its broader super app vision, focusing on user creativity and engagement.
This shift indicates OpenAI's commitment to enhancing its product lineup and staying competitive in AI-driven creativity.

stories.title: Leicester RELEGATED to L1; Coventry seal title & Millwall up to second - EFL LIVE!
bullets: Leicester City was relegated to League One on April 21, 2026, after a disappointing season in the EFL Championship.
Coventry City secured the Championship title, while Millwall climbed to second place, ensuring promotion to the Premier League.
Leicester's relegation marks a significant decline for the club, which previously enjoyed top-flight football for several years.

stories.title: The Best Samsung Galaxy Watch for Athletes and Adventurers Is Over $100 Off Right Now
bullets: The latest Samsung Galaxy Watch model is currently discounted by over $100, making it more accessible for athletes and adventurers.
This LTE model features double the storage capacity compared to its predecessor, enhancing user experience and functionality.
The price drop reflects Samsung's strategy to attract more customers in the competitive smartwatch market.

stories.title: The Peace That an Eclipse Brings
bullets: The total solar eclipse of 2024 created a moment of awe, impacting millions across North America on April 8, 2024.
Witnesses reported feelings of peace and wonder, as the eclipse temporarily darkened the sky and altered the atmosphere.
This event highlights the profound connection between celestial phenomena and human emotions, fostering a sense of unity.

stories.title: You've heard of the Space Force. Now there's a 'NASA Force' — but it's not about making space war
bullets: NASA has initiated the 'NASA Force' program to recruit industry talent for short-term roles, announced on April 21, 2026, post-Artemis 2 mission.
Applications are now open, aiming to bring fresh expertise into NASA's projects and enhance its capabilities in space exploration.
This initiative reflects NASA's commitment to innovation and collaboration with the private sector in advancing space missions.

stories.title: Bring nebulas and galaxies into focus with nearly $700 off this smart telescope
bullets: A smart telescope, the Unistellar Odyssey Pro, is now available at a $700 discount, appealing to amateur astronomers.
This telescope is designed to capture detailed images of nebulas and galaxies, even in light-polluted areas.
The price reduction aims to increase accessibility to advanced astrophotography tools for enthusiasts and hobbyists.

stories.title: ‘Beyond Inheritance’ offers a new view on mutations
bullets: Roxanne Khamsi's debut book, 'Beyond Inheritance,' presents a novel perspective on mutations, challenging traditional views on genetics.
The book explores mutations beyond the confines of birth and death, emphasizing their role in evolution and adaptation.
Khamsi's insights could reshape public understanding of genetics and its implications for health and disease.

stories.title: Investors are valuing Polymarket $7 billion less than archrival Kalshi—and crypto could be one reason why
bullets: Polymarket is currently valued at $15 billion, $7 billion less than its competitor Kalshi, which stands at $22 billion.
The valuation disparity is attributed to factors including market trends and the influence of cryptocurrency on investor sentiment.
This situation highlights the competitive landscape in the prediction market sector and the challenges Polymarket faces.

stories.title: A Game Of ‘Clue’ Turns Into Open Warfare
bullets: In a recent edition of the Defector Funbag, various topics were discussed, including juicers and airplane seat preparation.
The segment encourages reader interaction, inviting letters and opinions on diverse subjects, fostering community engagement.
This approach enhances the Funbag's appeal, making it a platform for lively discussions and reader contributions.

stories.title: The Supreme Court Just Twisted Its Cop Immunity Doctrine in an Even More Violent Direction
bullets: The Supreme Court's recent ruling has further expanded its cop immunity doctrine, following the death of Roy Scott, a 65-year-old Black man.
Scott called police for medical assistance but was killed during the encounter, raising concerns about police accountability.
This decision could have lasting implications for civil rights and police reform efforts across the United States.

stories.title: A timeline of Trump's shifting statements about how long the Iran war will last
bullets: Since February 28, 2026, President Donald Trump has made conflicting statements about the duration of the Iran war, declaring victory and issuing threats.
Trump's statements have included claims of a quick resolution and later suggestions of a prolonged conflict, causing confusion among allies and critics.
These contradictions could impact U.S. foreign relations and military strategy in the Middle East as tensions escalate.

stories.title: Save Money By Turning Your Xbox Expansion Card Into An SSD For Your PC
bullets: A new method allows Xbox expansion cards to be repurposed as SSDs for PCs, addressing the rising costs of fast storage amid the AI boom.
These cards, manufactured by Microsoft and Seagate, utilize CF Express Memory Card format, making them compatible with various devices.
This discovery could save gamers and PC users significant money, as SSD prices continue to increase due to high demand.

stories.title: Argentina posts ‘historic’ March trade surplus as exports surge
bullets: Argentina reported a historic trade surplus of $2.5 billion in March 2026, driven largely by a surge in energy exports.
The energy sector accounted for nearly half of this surplus, according to data released by the National Institute of Statistics and Censuses (INDEC).
This surplus may bolster Argentina's economy and improve its trade balance amid ongoing economic challenges.

stories.title: Valve's big Steam Deck update makes old hardware feel new
bullets: Valve released a significant update for the Steam Deck on April 21, 2026, enhancing the performance of its existing hardware.
The update focuses on improving the operating system, Steam OS, which stabilizes gameplay and optimizes performance for users.
This move aims to extend the lifespan of the Steam Deck and maintain its popularity in the competitive handheld gaming market.

stories.title: NASA shuts off another Voyager 1 instrument as humanity's most distant spacecraft prepares for risky 'Big Bang' maneuver to save power
bullets: NASA has turned off another instrument on Voyager 1 as it prepares for a critical 'Big Bang' maneuver to conserve power.
After nearly 50 years in space, both Voyager spacecraft are running low on nuclear power, necessitating these shutdowns.
This maneuver is crucial for extending the mission's lifespan, allowing Voyager 1 to continue sending data from the edge of the solar system.

stories.title: People keep trespassing near cave filled with bats infected by Ebola’s cousin
bullets: Trespassers continue to enter a cave in Africa that houses bats infected with the Marburg virus, a cousin of Ebola.
The Marburg virus has a mortality rate nearing 90%, raising public health concerns about potential outbreaks from human exposure.
Authorities are urging the public to avoid the area to prevent possible transmission and protect community health.

stories.title: Taiwan president blames China for forced cancellation of Eswatini trip
bullets: Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te canceled his trip to Eswatini on April 21, 2026, due to revoked overflight permissions.
The cancellations were attributed to intense pressure from China, affecting Taiwan's diplomatic engagements in Africa.
This incident highlights ongoing tensions between Taiwan and China, impacting Taiwan's international relations and visibility.

stories.title: SystemBC C2 Server Reveals 1,570+ Victims in The Gentlemen Ransomware Operation
bullets: The Gentlemen ransomware operation has been linked to over 1,570 victims through the SystemBC command-and-control server discovered on April 21, 2026.
Check Point's research indicates that the operation deploys proxy malware, significantly impacting organizations worldwide.
This revelation underscores the growing threat of ransomware and the need for enhanced cybersecurity measures across industries.

stories.title: Democratic Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick resigns just before House Ethics considers expulsion
bullets: Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick of Florida resigned on April 21, 2026, just before the House Ethics Committee was set to consider her expulsion.
Her resignation comes amid allegations of misconduct, with the committee's review indicating serious ethical concerns regarding her conduct.
This development may impact the Democratic Party's standing in Florida and prompt discussions about accountability in Congress.
